title: Kagenti’s Approach to Multi-Agent Security for AI Agents
summary: Kagenti proposes a multi-agent security approach for AI agents that uses identity-based security, delegation chains, and open source infrastructure. The method aims to prevent data leaks and invisible breaches in production agentic AI systems by securing multi-agent interactions.
writeup: The video presents Kagenti, a multi-agent security approach for AI agents built on identity-based security, delegation chains, and open source infrastructure. It addresses the risk that production agentic AI systems can leak data or suffer invisible breaches when multi-agent interactions go unsecured. The core takeaways: securing agent-to-agent interactions through verifiable identity, tracking authority across delegation chains, and applying zero-trust principles to keep agent activity from going unmonitored.
